channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Ritzy Florida enclave becomes Chinese smuggling hot spot as former military identifies driving force
item.description: Law enforcement in an affluent suburban area of South Florida have observed a growing trend of Chinese migrants arriving via human smuggling operations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge weighs request to withhold investigation records in deaths of Gene Hackman and wife
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — A New Mexico court is weighing whether to block the disclosure of an array of records from an investigation into the deaths of actor Gene Hackman and his wife, Betsy Arakawa, at the request of the couple’s estate.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Helicopter lands on street, causing traffic backup on I-15 in San Diego County
item.description: A helicopter with engine failure forced a pilot to land on a side street along on I-15 in unincorporated Escondido, causing traffic to slow to a standstill Sunday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, KTLA 5

item.title: How a new Georgia bill could change the fate of domestic abuse survivors in prison
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Mary Favors is still plagued by nightmares from the days her husband beat her, choked her and verbally and sexually abused her. Now, she is in prison for killing him. Their disagreements often turned physical, and five times he was convicted of abusing her.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran rejects direct negotiations with the US in response to Trump's letter
item.description: Iran's president said Sunday that the Islamic Republic rejected direct negotiations with the United States over its rapidly advancing nuclear program, offering Tehran's first response to a letter President Donald Trump sent to the country's supreme leader.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge officially clears Joshua Riibe in Pitt student Sudiksha Konanki’s spring break DR disappearance
item.description: A Dominican judge overseeing the case involving the missing University of Pittsburgh college student has released his reasoning for his final ruling against man believed to see her alive last.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Authorities investigate damaging fire at New Mexico's GOP headquarters
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, New Mexico (AP) — Federal authorities are investigating a fire early Sunday that damaged the entryway to the headquarters of the Republican Party of New Mexico in Albuquerque.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Figure skater who lost both parents in DC plane crash brings world championships crowd to its feet
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Maxim Naumov came to a stop in the middle of the ice, looked up at the sky and patted his heart. Then he mouthed a few words, in Russian, to his parents: “This is for you guys. You guys are with me. I love you both.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: RECALL: Thousands of pounds of Our Family coffee mislabeled
item.description: The FDA lists this as a class II recall, in which use or exposure to a product “may cause temporary or medically reversible adverse health consequences or where the probability of serious adverse health consequences is remote.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Utah woman receives 100 cancer treatments, living far beyond doctors' expectations
item.description: An 84-year-old Utah woman recently received her 100th cancer treatment, outliving doctors' expectations.
item.dc:creator: Yeonseung Kim, KRON4

item.title: Minnesota officials seek answers in case of graduate student detained by ICE
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Officials in Minnesota are seeking answers in the case of a University of Minnesota graduate student who’s being detained by U.S. immigration authorities for unknown reasons.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Statham’s 'A Working Man’ upsets ‘Snow White’ to take No. 1 at the box office
item.description: In an unexpected upset, the Jason Statham thriller “A Working Man” took No. 1 at the box office, besting the rapidly declining “Snow White,” according to studio estimates Sunday.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Planning to visit these national parks? You may need a reservation to get in
item.description: While hundreds of parks are free to enter, others use timed entry and other reservation methods to control crowds — but that also means you may need to secure your spot well in advance.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Rain turns to ice, cuts power in Great Lakes region while Tennessee braces for wild weather
item.description: Thousands of people have no power in Wisconsin, Michigan and Indiana after freezing rain or severe storms brought down trees and power lines.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump 'very angry' at Putin's remarks on Zelensky
item.description: The president threatened to slap a new tariff on Russia if it is at fault for stalling an end "to bloodshed.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Delta Air Lines drops new set of pilot trading cards: How to get yours
item.description: Trading card collectors and aviation fanatics will soon have an exclusive set of cards to begin collecting on Delta Air Lines flights this year.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, KRON4

item.title: Myanmar quake death toll rises to 1,644 as resistance movement announces partial ceasefire
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A unilateral partial ceasefire to facilitate earthquake relief efforts was announced on Saturday by Myanmar’s shadow National Unity Government, which coordinates the popular struggle against the ruling military. The country's death toll from the disaster soared to 1,644.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Walter Clayton Jr. rallies Florida into Final Four with 84-79 March Madness win over Texas Tech
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Coach Todd Golden cut the final strand of the net, waved it to the crowd and performed the famed Gator Chomp. A celebration that seemed completely improbable just a few minutes earlier was in high gear. Florida is heading back to the Final Four. Walter Clayton Jr.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bueckers has career-high 40 to lead one-woman March Madness rout for UConn, 82-59 over Oklahoma
item.description: Paige Bueckers scored a career-high 40 points, rescuing UConn from its first-half doldrums and single-handedly turning a tight game against Oklahoma into an 82-59 rout on Saturday that sent the Huskies to the Elite Eight of the women's NCAA Tournament.
item.dc:creator: ANNE M. PETERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Who is Trevor Milton, the startup founder and donor pardoned by Trump
item.description: Milton said Thursday in a post on social platform X that he spoke to Trump over the phone and that he signed his pardon of “innocence.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Myanmar's earthquake death toll jumps to 1,644 as more bodies are recovered from the rubble
item.description: Myanmar’s ruling military confirmed death toll from a devastating 7.7 magnitude earthquake rose to 1,644, as more bodies were pulled from the rubble of the scores of buildings that collapsed.
item.dc:creator: David Rising, KTLA 5

item.title: White House may change how journalists can ask Trump questions
item.description: The White House is currently locked in a legal battle with the Associated Press over its decision to ban the wire service from key West Wing spaces over its “Gulf of America” stylebook policy.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, WGN 9

item.title: Comedian Paul Rodriguez arrested on drug charge in California, police say
item.description: Rodriguez, 70, was booked at the Burbank Police Jail for narcotics possession and later released with a citation to appear in court on April 25, Sgt. Turner elaborated.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, Fox 5

item.title: Steph Curry scores a 3-book deal, starting with a 'personal reflection' on his life and career
item.description: The NBA superstar has agreed to release three books through One World, beginning Sept. 9 with “Shot Ready," which Curry is calling a “personal reflection” on his life on and off the court. The book also includes more than 100 photographs.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Passenger flight and Air Force jet diverted from potential collision at DC airport
item.description: A U.S. passenger flight preparing to leave the nation's capital and an incoming military jet received instructions to divert and prevent a possible collision, officials said. Delta Air Lines Flight 2983 was cleared for takeoff at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport on Friday around 3:15 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Patient dies after getting rabies from organ transplant
item.description: Michigan Department of Health and Human Services spokesperson Lynn Sutfin said there is no threat to the general public.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Columbia student sues Trump admin over efforts to deport her
item.description: Columbia University student Yunseo Chung is suing the Trump administration amid its efforts to deport the 21-year-old, who has been in the U.S. since she was 7 and has lawful permanent resident status.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9
